item.title: Google, Accel India accelerator chooses 5 startups and none are ‘AI wrappers’
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Google and Accel say about 70% of AI startup pitches tied to India were "wrappers" as they reviewed more than 4,000 applications for their Atoms cohort.

item.title: ByteDance reportedly pauses global launch of its Seedance 2.0 video generator
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The company is reportedly delaying the launch as its engineers and lawyers work to avert further legal issues.

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: Wiz investor unpacks Google’s $32B acquisition
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Shardul Shah of Index Ventures walks us through Google's biggest acquisition ever.

item.title: US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The Army described this as a single enterprise contract consolidating more than 120 separate "procurement actions."

item.title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

item.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

item.title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The AI lab is revamping its effort to build an AI coding tool, with two new executives joining from Cursor.

item.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

item.title: Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

item.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

item.title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Spotify will let you edit your Taste Profile to control your recommendations
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: When you edit your Taste Profile, you'll impact your personalized playlists like Discover Weekly, recommendations, and Wrapped.

item.title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: According to Index Ventures Partner Shardul Shah, cybersecurity startup Wiz sits “at the center of three tailwinds: AI, cloud, and security spend.” Those tailwinds powered what just became the largest venture-backed acquisition in history — Google’s $32 billion deal, finalized after a declined 2024 offer, antitrust review on both sides of the Atlantic, and an […]

item.title: Peacock expands into AI-driven video, mobile-first live sports, and gaming
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Peacock is betting on new AI-powered video experiences, vertical clips, and mobile games to help its growth.

item.title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: After selling his AI startup to AMD for $665 million, Peter Sarlin is back with Qutwo, a new venture building the infrastructure it believes enterprises will need when quantum computing finally arrives.

item.title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Caller identity platform Truecaller recently launched a new feature that lets one person become an admin of a family group, get alerts about fraud calls received by other members, and even end a call on their behalf if they suspect a family member might get scammed.

item.title: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: GTC — which stands for GPU Technology Conference — is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically uses the spotlight to announce new products, champion partnerships, and lay out its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role in the future of computing and AI.

item.title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.
